basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Aḥzāb — “The Confederates” — opens with the command addressed to the Prophet to fear God, to obey neither the disbelievers nor the hypocrites, and to place everything in the divine hands: “God suffices as a Trustee.” Qushayrī makes tawakkul the beating heart of the sūra: trusting reliance is the equanimity of the heart in lack and in abundance. Then comes the account of the Trench, when the Confederate armies besieged Medina and God sent against them a hurricane and invisible hosts; the hypocrites trembled, but the believers saw in it only an increase of faith and submission. The sūra raises the figure of the Prophet as “a beautiful example” (uswa ḥasana), institutes the rules of the Household, and makes the Prophet's wives the Mothers of the Believers. It culminates on two mysteries: the Trust (amāna) offered to the heavens, the earth, and the mountains, which were afraid of it and declined it out of reverence, while man bore it — a refusal of reverent awe, not of pride, says Qushayrī, and it is by God, not by himself, that man bore it; and the blessing of God and His angels upon the Prophet, sealed by the call to abundant remembrance: “remember God with much remembrance and glorify Him morning and evening,” for the remembrance one can sustain unceasingly is that of the heart.
themes: the fear of God and trusting reliance
the Battle of the Trench and the invisible hosts
the Mothers of the Believers and the Household
the blessing upon the Prophet and abundant remembrance

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ اتَّقِ اللَّهَ وَلَا تُطِعِ الْكَافِرِينَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu ttaqi llāha wa-lā tuṭiʿi l-kāfirīna wa-l-munāfiqīna inna llāha kāna ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: O Prophet! Fear God, and obey neither the disbelievers nor the hypocrites. God is, in truth, all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears in the address “O Prophet” an unfolding of titles of honor: O you whose state is ennobled, whose rank is magnified with Us, O you raised to the highest degrees by the most sublime nearness, O you the confidant of Our secrets, the bearer of Our speech to Our beloved. And “fear God” takes on an inner sense: beware of regarding any other beside Us, of finding rest in anything apart from Us, of affirming any but Us. For the fear of God (taqwā) is a sentinel posted over the hearts of His friends, preventing them, in their breaths and stillnesses, from looking toward any other than Him.

versets.ar: وَاتَّبِعْ مَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-ttabiʿ mā yūḥā ilayka min rabbika inna llāha kāna bimā taʿmalūna khabīran
versets.trad: Follow what is revealed to you from your Lord. God is perfectly aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Follow and do not innovate; conform to what We command you, and do not let yourself be guided by your own choice where We choose for you. Qushayrī urges the servant to dwell neither in the lands of sloth nor on the slope of negligence: be Ours and not your own, stand by Us and not by yourself. To follow, here, is the effacement of one's own will before the divine will.

mots.def: khabīr: who knows the depth of things.

versets.ar: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-tawakkal ʿalā llāhi wa-kafā bi-llāhi wakīlan
versets.trad: Rely on God! God suffices as a Trustee.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this verse the summit of abandonment. Tawakkul is realization, then character, then firm assurance, then loving effacement: one is realized in belief, clothed in the Law, made firm in the sharing of the decree, effaced in pure servitude before Him. And again: tawakkul is the equanimity of the heart in lack and in abundance. Whoever truly relies neither dreads deprivation nor is intoxicated by possession.

mots.def: wakīl: the one to whom one entrusts one's affairs.

versets.ar: مَّا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لِرَجُلٍ مِّن قَلْبَيْنِ فِي جَوْفِهِ ۚ وَمَا جَعَلَ أَزْوَاجَكُمُ اللَّائِي تُظَاهِرُونَ مِنْهُنَّ أُمَّهَاتِكُمْ ۚ وَمَا جَعَلَ أَدْعِيَاءَكُمْ أَبْنَاءَكُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ قَوْلُكُم بِأَفْوَاهِكُمْ ۖ وَاللَّهُ يَقُولُ الْحَقَّ وَهُوَ يَهْدِي السَّبِيلَ
versets.tr: mā jaʿala llāhu li-rajulin min qalbayni fī jawfihi wa-mā jaʿala azwājakumu l-lāʾī tuẓāhirūna minhunna ummahātikum wa-mā jaʿala adʿiyāʾakum abnāʾakum dhālikum qawlukum bi-afwāhikum wa-llāhu yaqūlu l-ḥaqqa wa-huwa yahdī s-sabīla
versets.trad: God has not placed two hearts within the breast of any man; nor has He made your wives, whom you repudiate by ẓihār, your mothers; nor has He made your adopted sons your own sons. These are but words from your mouths; but God speaks the Truth and He guides upon the path.
versets.commentaire: The heart, says Qushayrī, cannot serve two masters: when the heart busies itself with one thing, it absents itself from all else; whoever is occupied with what belongs to nothingness is severed from the One whose attribute is eternity. One heart, then one direction: night and day do not meet, the Unseen and the other-than-Him do not converge. As for the tongue's assimilations — wife turned into mother, adopted son into son — these are only your words returned upon yourselves, void of reality before the Truth.

mots.def: ẓihār: a man's formula likening his wife to his mother.

versets.ar: ادْعُوهُمْ لِآبَائِهِمْ هُوَ أَقْسَطُ عِندَ اللَّهِ ۚ فَإِن لَّمْ تَعْلَمُوا آبَاءَهُمْ فَإِخْوَانُكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ وَمَوَالِيكُمْ ۚ وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ فِيمَا أَخْطَأْتُم بِهِ وَلَٰكِن مَّا تَعَمَّدَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: udʿūhum li-ābāʾihim huwa aqsaṭu ʿinda llāhi fa-in lam taʿlamū ābāʾahum fa-ikhwānukum fī d-dīni wa-mawālīkum wa-laysa ʿalaykum junāḥun fīmā akhṭaʾtum bihi wa-lākin mā taʿammadat qulūbukum wa-kāna llāhu ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: Call them by their fathers' names: that is more just before God; but if you do not know their fathers, they are your brothers in religion and your wards. There is no blame upon you for what you did in error, but only for what your hearts premeditated. God is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: When the bond of blood is lacking, says Qushayrī, the brotherhood of religion gathers you; and the kinship of faith and affinity outweighs the kinship of blood. He cites these verses: “They claimed that nearness lies in father and uncles — and I say: the brothers of purity are the true kin; we are akin to them in form, knowledge, and affection, though we be distant in our origins.” Thus true lineage is that of hearts in accord.

versets.ar: النَّبِيُّ أَوْلَىٰ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ وَأَزْوَاجُهُ أُمَّهَاتُهُمْ ۗ وَأُولُو الْأَرْحَامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلَىٰ بِبَعْضٍ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُهَاجِرِينَ إِلَّا أَن تَفْعَلُوا إِلَىٰ أَوْلِيَائِكُم مَّعْرُوفًا ۚ كَانَ ذَٰلِكَ فِي الْكِتَابِ مَسْطُورًا
versets.tr: an-nabiyyu awlā bi-l-muʾminīna min anfusihim wa-azwājuhu ummahātuhum wa-ulū l-arḥāmi baʿḍuhum awlā bi-baʿḍin fī kitābi llāhi mina l-muʾminīna wa-l-muhājirīna illā an tafʿalū ilā awliyāʾikum maʿrūfan kāna dhālika fī l-kitābi masṭūran
versets.trad: The Prophet is closer to the believers than they are to one another, and his wives are their mothers. Among blood relations, some have, according to the Book of God, priority [in inheritance] over the believers and the emigrants, unless you wish to treat your protégés generously. This is inscribed in the Book.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from this verse an inner indication: to place his way before your passion, to halt at his indication rather than at what your desire craves, to prefer the one who joins you to God, by cause or by kinship, over your dearest kin. And on “the kindred by blood”: let strangers stay aside and let your bond be with the near — but maintaining kinship is not a matter of neighboring dwellings; it is the accord of hearts and mutual aid in trial as in joy: “our souls are in a single place, though our bodies be in Syria or Khurāsān.”

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِيثَاقَهُمْ وَمِنكَ وَمِن نُّوحٍ وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ ۖ وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُم مِّيثَاقًا غَلِيظًا
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadhnā mina n-nabiyyīna mīthāqahum wa-minka wa-min nūḥin wa-ibrāhīma wa-mūsā wa-ʿīsā bni maryama wa-akhadhnā minhum mīthāqan ghalīẓan
versets.trad: When We made the covenant with the Prophets, and with you, and with Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus son of Mary — it was a solemn covenant that We made with them
versets.commentaire: The pact of the prophets, Qushayrī explains, was taken at the very drawing-forth of the progeny from Adam's loins — that is the first covenant — then renewed at the sending of each messenger. But he distinguishes our Prophet: God elected him and made him hear His speech without intermediary, on the night of the ascension; Moses too received the pact without intermediary, but our Prophet had, along with the hearing of the discourse, the unveiling of the vision. Beyond the prophets, God also takes the covenant of the servants through their hearts and their secrets, each according to what he is made capable of.

versets.ar: لِّيَسْأَلَ الصَّادِقِينَ عَن صِدْقِهِمْ ۚ وَأَعَدَّ لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا
versets.tr: li-yasʾala ṣ-ṣādiqīna ʿan ṣidqihim wa-aʿadda li-l-kāfirīna ʿadhāban alīman
versets.trad: so that God may question the truthful about their truthfulness; and He has prepared for the disbelievers a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: This questioning, Qushayrī specifies, is a question of honor and not of reproach, a question that elevates and does not crush. Then he defines truthfulness by degrees: ṣidq is that there be no admixture in your states, no doubt in your belief, no flaw in your works. Truthfulness in trusting reliance, he adds, is not to be troubled by deprivation and not to be intoxicated by possession; and the ultimate truthfulness is the equality of the manifest and the hidden.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ جَاءَتْكُمْ جُنُودٌ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًا وَجُنُودًا لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum idh jāʾatkum junūdun fa-arsalnā ʿalayhim rīḥan wa-junūdan lam tarawhā wa-kāna llāhu bimā taʿmalūna baṣīran
versets.trad: O you who believe, remember God's blessing upon you: when armies bore down upon you, We sent against them a hurricane and invisible hosts. God saw perfectly what you were doing!
versets.commentaire: To remember God's blessing, says Qushayrī, is to meet it with gratitude; if you recalled what was averted from you in the past, the present trial would be light upon you; if you recalled what He granted you before, trust in what you hope for the future would draw near to your heart. And here he unveils the “blessings of withholding”: how many trials He turned away from the servant without his knowing, how many affairs He hindered while the servant lamented — knowing, He, that to ease them would have been his ruin; He withheld them out of mercy, and the servant suspects Him and is anguished!

versets.ar: إِذْ جَاءُوكُم مِّن فَوْقِكُمْ وَمِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ وَإِذْ زَاغَتِ الْأَبْصَارُ وَبَلَغَتِ الْقُلُوبُ الْحَنَاجِرَ وَتَظُنُّونَ بِاللَّهِ الظُّنُونَا
versets.tr: idh jāʾūkum min fawqikum wa-min asfala minkum wa-idh zāghati l-abṣāru wa-balaghati l-qulūbu l-ḥanājira wa-taẓunnūna bi-llāhi ẓ-ẓunūnā
versets.trad: When the enemies bore down on you from above and from below, when your eyes rolled back, when hearts rose to the throats and you entertained vain conjectures about God,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the distress of the encircled believer: the canopies of trial enveloped them, the enemy host surrounded them, hearts rose to the throats, conjectures divided, the folds of doubt crept into them, and the wandering of suspicion appeared in their inmost depths. The next verse will be its resolution: then He dispelled all that from them, and made the springs of their serenity gush forth. The extreme vise is the threshold where the divine tranquility breaks in.

mots.def: zāgha: to deviate, to be troubled.

versets.ar: هُنَالِكَ ابْتُلِيَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَزُلْزِلُوا زِلْزَالًا شَدِيدًا
versets.tr: hunālika btuliya l-muʾminūna wa-zulzilū zilzālan shadīdan
versets.trad: there the believers were tried and violently shaken.
versets.commentaire: The trial, says Qushayrī in continuation of his commentary, was the crucible in which the metal of hearts appeared. Then He removed all that distress from them, dispelled its severity, scattered their cloud, drove their anxieties from their hearts, and made the springs of their serenity gush forth. The violent shaking is not punishment but purifying trial: it separates the sincere from the dissembler and prepares the heart to receive the sakīna.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ يَقُولُ الْمُنَافِقُونَ وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ مَّا وَعَدَنَا اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ إِلَّا غُرُورًا
versets.tr: wa-idh yaqūlu l-munāfiqūna wa-lladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun mā waʿadanā llāhu wa-rasūluhu illā ghurūran
versets.trad: When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a disease said: “God and His Messenger promised us only to delude us,”
versets.commentaire: They openly proclaimed the denial their hearts concealed, as soon as they found in speech a space to speak. Qushayrī notes that the trial brings to the surface what lay hidden: the disease of the heart, long concealed, speaks the moment fear opens its mouth. The siege created nothing in them: it only revealed.

mots.def: maraḍ: sickness of the heart, doubt.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَت طَّائِفَةٌ مِّنْهُمْ يَا أَهْلَ يَثْرِبَ لَا مُقَامَ لَكُمْ فَارْجِعُوا ۚ وَيَسْتَأْذِنُ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمُ النَّبِيَّ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّ بُيُوتَنَا عَوْرَةٌ وَمَا هِيَ بِعَوْرَةٍ ۖ إِن يُرِيدُونَ إِلَّا فِرَارًا
versets.tr: wa-idh qālat ṭāʾifatun minhum yā ahla yathriba lā muqāma lakum fa-rjiʿū wa-yastaʾdhinu farīqun minhumu n-nabiyya yaqūlūna inna buyūtanā ʿawratun wa-mā hiya bi-ʿawratin in yurīdūna illā firāran
versets.trad: and when some of them said: “O people of Yathrib (Medina)! There is no place for you here: so return!” — and a group of them asked the Prophet's leave to withdraw, saying: “Our houses are left undefended” — yet they were not threatened; they wished only to flee.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks the pretext: they excused themselves on the destitution of their houses and the loss of their goods, lying in the excuse they paraded; nothing drove them to act so but their cowardice and the weakness of their certainty. The commentator targets in passing those who, on the Path, take refuge in convenient pretexts and the claims of the ego: fear always finds an alibi.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ دُخِلَتْ عَلَيْهِم مِّنْ أَقْطَارِهَا ثُمَّ سُئِلُوا الْفِتْنَةَ لَآتَوْهَا وَمَا تَلَبَّثُوا بِهَا إِلَّا يَسِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-law dukhilat ʿalayhim min aqṭārihā thumma suʾilū l-fitnata la-ātawhā wa-mā talabbathū bihā illā yasīran
versets.trad: Had the enemy made a breach in certain points of the city and had they then been asked to betray, they would have consented readily and without delay.
themes: the fragility of the sick heart
versets.commentaire: The heart that has not taken root in certainty yields at the first assault. Whoever is not made firm by God betrays as soon as pressure reaches him: barely solicited, the hypocrite would have surrendered his faith “without delay,” so emptied of all firmness had the love of this world left him. Sincerity, by contrast, is the root that holds when the storm tries the tree.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ كَانُوا عَاهَدُوا اللَّهَ مِن قَبْلُ لَا يُوَلُّونَ الْأَدْبَارَ ۚ وَكَانَ عَهْدُ اللَّهِ مَسْئُولًا
versets.tr: wa-laqad kānū ʿāhadū llāha min qablu lā yuwallūna l-adbāra wa-kāna ʿahdu llāhi masʾūlan
versets.trad: Yet they had earlier pledged to God that they would not turn their backs. And the pledge made to God will be asked of.
versets.commentaire: But when the matter became serious and the trial pressing, truthfulness did not second them; they forgot that they would be asked about their pledge and punished for the sin they had committed. Qushayrī makes the broken pact the counter-example of the truthful: the promise made to God is not a word but a debt of one's very being, whose fidelity is measured at the hour of danger.

mots.def: wallā: to turn away, to flee.
masʾūl: that for which one is questioned.

versets.ar: قُل لَّن يَنفَعَكُمُ الْفِرَارُ إِن فَرَرْتُم مِّنَ الْمَوْتِ أَوِ الْقَتْلِ وَإِذًا لَّا تُمَتَّعُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: qul lan yanfaʿakumu l-firāru in farartum mina l-mawti awi l-qatli wa-idhan lā tumattaʿūna illā qalīlan
versets.trad: Say: “Flight will avail you nothing if you flee from death or slaughter; your enjoyment will then be but brief.”
versets.commentaire: For the appointed terms admit neither delay nor advance. Qushayrī cites the adage: “whoever flees what must come writhes in the palm of the One who pursues him.” And on the “brief enjoyment”: what the servant hoards away from God — wealth, rank, precious thing, or kin — is not blessed to him; he finds in it no protection and no lasting joy. To flee the decree is to flee toward the very hand that holds the decree.

versets.ar: قُلْ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَعْصِمُكُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ إِنْ أَرَادَ بِكُمْ سُوءًا أَوْ أَرَادَ بِكُمْ رَحْمَةً ۚ وَلَا يَجِدُونَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: qul man dhā lladhī yaʿṣimukum mina llāhi in arāda bikum sūʾan aw arāda bikum raḥmatan wa-lā yajidūna lahum min dūni llāhi waliyyan wa-lā naṣīran
versets.trad: Say: “Who then will shield you from God, should He will for you an ill or will for you a mercy?” They will find, apart from God, neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the verse into two unanswerable questions: who then would obtain for you, apart from Him, an object of hope? And who would turn away from you, apart from Him, an enemy? There is no good to attain nor ill to avert that does not pass through His sole will; to seek a protector apart from Him is to seek a support in the void.

mots.sens: will shield you, put beyond reach

versets.ar: قَدْ يَعْلَمُ اللَّهُ الْمُعَوِّقِينَ مِنكُمْ وَالْقَائِلِينَ لِإِخْوَانِهِمْ هَلُمَّ إِلَيْنَا ۖ وَلَا يَأْتُونَ الْبَأْسَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: qad yaʿlamu llāhu l-muʿawwiqīna minkum wa-l-qāʾilīna li-ikhwānihim halumma ilaynā wa-lā yaʾtūna l-baʾsa illā qalīlan
versets.trad: God knows those among you who preach defeatism and who say to their brothers: “Come to us!” while they themselves show little ardor for battle,
versets.commentaire: These are the ones who held themselves back from aiding the Prophet and held others back, to swell their number and make their scheme more hidden — not knowing that God informs His Messenger of them. Qushayrī underscores the paradox of the dissembler: he believes his intrigue veiled, while nothing escapes the divine gaze, which enlightens His Prophet upon the folds of hearts.

versets.ar: أَشِحَّةً عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ فَإِذَا جَاءَ الْخَوْفُ رَأَيْتَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ تَدُورُ أَعْيُنُهُمْ كَالَّذِي يُغْشَىٰ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْمَوْتِ ۖ فَإِذَا ذَهَبَ الْخَوْفُ سَلَقُوكُم بِأَلْسِنَةٍ حِدَادٍ أَشِحَّةً عَلَى الْخَيْرِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا فَأَحْبَطَ اللَّهُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرًا
versets.tr: ashiḥḥatan ʿalaykum fa-idhā jāʾa l-khawfu raʾaytahum yanẓurūna ilayka tadūru aʿyunuhum ka-lladhī yughshā ʿalayhi mina l-mawti fa-idhā dhahaba l-khawfu salaqūkum bi-alsinatin ḥidādin ashiḥḥatan ʿalā l-khayri ulāʾika lam yuʾminū fa-aḥbaṭa llāhu aʿmālahum wa-kāna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīran
versets.trad: and stint on the help they owe you. But when fear comes, you see them looking at you, their eyes rolling like one fainting in death; then, once the fear has passed, they assail you with sharp tongues, stinting on the good. These have not believed, so God will make their works vain: that is easy for God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts their two faces: when fear comes, terror unhinges their reason, their gazes swirl, all their limbs are paralyzed and refuse to help; but once the fear has passed, they adorn their speech, deploy their deceit, and scheme in the baseness of their rancors. Such are those whose hearts faith never touched, nor made sincere the declarations they paraded.

mots.def: salaqa: to attack with harsh words.

versets.ar: يَحْسَبُونَ الْأَحْزَابَ لَمْ يَذْهَبُوا ۖ وَإِن يَأْتِ الْأَحْزَابُ يَوَدُّوا لَوْ أَنَّهُم بَادُونَ فِي الْأَعْرَابِ يَسْأَلُونَ عَنْ أَنبَائِكُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ كَانُوا فِيكُم مَّا قَاتَلُوا إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: yaḥsabūna l-aḥzāba lam yadhhabū wa-in yaʾti l-aḥzābu yawaddū law annahum bādūna fī l-aʿrābi yasʾalūna ʿan anbāʾikum wa-law kānū fīkum mā qātalū illā qalīlan
versets.trad: They think the Confederates have not departed. And were the Confederates to return, they would wish to withdraw to the desert among the Bedouins; they would then ask for news of you; and even if they remained among you, they would fight but feebly.
versets.commentaire: They imagine the Confederates have not departed, dread their return, and take fright at the shadow of themselves. Qushayrī paints the soul gnawed by fright, which projects everywhere the enemy it fears; when attachment to life has emptied the heart, even presence does not make the fighter. Fear is the prison the coward builds for himself.

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ لِّمَن كَانَ يَرْجُو اللَّهَ وَالْيَوْمَ الْآخِرَ وَذَكَرَ اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا
versets.tr: laqad kāna lakum fī rasūli llāhi uswatun ḥasanatun li-man kāna yarjū llāha wa-l-yawma l-ākhira wa-dhakara llāha kathīran
versets.trad: You have in the Messenger of God a beautiful example for whoever hopes in God and the Last Day and remembers God much.
versets.commentaire: You have in the Messenger of God a beautiful example: he is your model, and it is incumbent upon you to follow him in all he traces for you. Qushayrī then distinguishes two registers: the words and acts of the Messenger are binding so long as no indication particularizes them; but his inner states none can contemplate — and if something of them shows through, it is, where he acquired them, an obligation like his acts; where they are not acquired, a singularity proper to him that none could claim to equal, so high is his rank.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا رَأَى الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الْأَحْزَابَ قَالُوا هَٰذَا مَا وَعَدَنَا اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَصَدَقَ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ ۚ وَمَا زَادَهُمْ إِلَّا إِيمَانًا وَتَسْلِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-lammā raʾā l-muʾminūna l-aḥzāba qālū hādhā mā waʿadanā llāhu wa-rasūluhu wa-ṣadaqa llāhu wa-rasūluhu wa-mā zādahum illā īmānan wa-taslīman
versets.trad: When the believers saw the Confederates, they said: “This is what God and His Messenger promised us! God and His Messenger spoke the truth!” And it only increased them in faith and submission.
versets.commentaire: Here is the exact reverse of the hypocrites' fate: just as the hypocrites' beliefs faltered at the sight of the enemies, the believers and the people of certainty redoubled in confidence, in boldness before the adversary, in submission to God's decree, and in strength come from God. The same trial that shakes the sick soul makes firm the sincere heart: what undoes one builds up the other.

versets.ar: مِّنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ رِجَالٌ صَدَقُوا مَا عَاهَدُوا اللَّهَ عَلَيْهِ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّن قَضَىٰ نَحْبَهُ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَنتَظِرُ ۖ وَمَا بَدَّلُوا تَبْدِيلًا
versets.tr: mina l-muʾminīna rijālun ṣadaqū mā ʿāhadū llāha ʿalayhi fa-minhum man qaḍā naḥbahu wa-minhum man yantaẓiru wa-mā baddalū tabdīlan
versets.trad: Among the believers are men who were faithful to the pact they had made with God. Of them, one has fulfilled the term of his life, and another still waits; but they have not wavered in any way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī praises their steadfastness in the trial: He thanked their conduct in battle, extolled their certainty at the sight of peril, and named them “men” to affirm the singularity of their rank and distinguish them from their peers by the elevation of their state. Then he defines ṣidq: the reality of truthfulness is to keep the pact and not overstep the limit; it is also said: the equality of the manifest and the secret; and again: firmness at the hour when the matter becomes serious.

versets.ar: لِّيَجْزِيَ اللَّهُ الصَّادِقِينَ بِصِدْقِهِمْ وَيُعَذِّبَ الْمُنَافِقِينَ إِن شَاءَ أَوْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: li-yajziya llāhu ṣ-ṣādiqīna bi-ṣidqihim wa-yuʿadhdhiba l-munāfiqīna in shāʾa aw yatūba ʿalayhim inna llāha kāna ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: Thus God rewards the truthful for their truthfulness and punishes, if He wills, the hypocrites, or accepts their repentance. Indeed, God is forgiving, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: In this world, He rewards the truthful with steadfastness, victory over the enemy, and the raising of the banner; in the next, with the beauty of the wage, the abundance of the return, and eternity in bliss. And on the suspended fate of the hypocrites, Qushayrī opens a door of hope: since He did not settle the punishment of the hypocrite but bound the verdict to hope, all the more fitting that He not disappoint the believer in his hope.

mots.def: tāba ʿalā: to turn toward, to grant repentance.

versets.ar: وَرَدَّ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِغَيْظِهِمْ لَمْ يَنَالُوا خَيْرًا ۚ وَكَفَى اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الْقِتَالَ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ قَوِيًّا عَزِيزًا
versets.tr: wa-radda llāhu lladhīna kafarū bi-ghayẓihim lam yanālū khayran wa-kafā llāhu l-muʾminīna l-qitāla wa-kāna llāhu qawiyyan ʿazīzan
versets.trad: God turned back those who disbelieved with their rage; they obtained no good; God sufficed the believers in the fighting; God is strong, mighty.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the divine sufficiency: He did not gladden the enemy at the expense of the Muslims, let no harm reach them from their scheme, turned their plot back upon their own throats, and uprooted them to the trunk. And the trial had its revealing function: thereby He brought into the open the jewels of their sincerity and their insincerity, thanking the one who deserved gratitude, exposing the one who deserved blame among the impostors.

versets.ar: وَأَنزَلَ الَّذِينَ ظَاهَرُوهُم مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مِن صَيَاصِيهِمْ وَقَذَفَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الرُّعْبَ فَرِيقًا تَقْتُلُونَ وَتَأْسِرُونَ فَرِيقًا
versets.tr: wa-anzala lladhīna ẓāharūhum min ahli l-kitābi min ṣayāṣīhim wa-qadhafa fī qulūbihimu r-ruʿba farīqan taqtulūna wa-taʾsirūna farīqan
versets.trad: And He brought down from their fortresses those of the People of the Book who had sided with the Confederates. He cast terror into their hearts. A part of them you slew, and another part you took captive.
versets.commentaire: When the Truth abridges, He perfects; when He heals, He suffices; when He fulfills, He fills to overflowing. Qushayrī sees in the fall of the fortresses the signature of the divine sufficiency: He made the Muslims victorious, made them inherit the strongholds, abased the arrogant, and sufficed them in every matter. The terror cast into the enemy's hearts is no human force but a divine descent.

versets.ar: وَأَوْرَثَكُمْ أَرْضَهُمْ وَدِيَارَهُمْ وَأَمْوَالَهُمْ وَأَرْضًا لَّمْ تَطَئُوهَا ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-awrathakum arḍahum wa-diyārahum wa-amwālahum wa-arḍan lam taṭaʾūhā wa-kāna llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīran
versets.trad: And He gave you in inheritance their land, their dwellings, their wealth, and a land your feet had never trodden. God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: The promised inheritance is not merely war spoil but a sign of the divine munificence: He sufficed them in every way in their matter, gave them power over their enemies, and made them inherit their dwellings. For Qushayrī, to receive “a land your feet had never trodden” bespeaks the generosity that outstrips merit — God gives beyond what the hand has conquered.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَاجِكَ إِن كُنتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتَهَا فَتَعَالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu qul li-azwājika in kuntunna turidna l-ḥayāta d-dunyā wa-zīnatahā fa-taʿālayna umattiʿkunna wa-usarriḥkunna sarāḥan jamīlan
versets.trad: O Prophet! Say to your wives: “If you desire the life of this world and its splendor, come: I will grant you some provision and give you an honorable release.
themes: the choice offered to the wives
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in this verse the Prophet's delicate concern: he wished that no heart, among the believing men and women, be occupied on his account, nor that any burden come back to him; so he left his wives the choice. And God, he adds, granted ʿĀʾisha, mother of the believers, to testify to the sincerity of her heart and the fullness of her faith, by choosing God and His Messenger; the others followed her way.

mots.racine: س ر ح / ج م ل

versets.ar: وَإِن كُنتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِنَاتِ مِنكُنَّ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-in kuntunna turidna llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-d-dāra l-ākhirata fa-inna llāha aʿadda li-l-muḥsināti minkunna ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: And if you desire God, His Messenger, and the abode of the Hereafter, then know that God has prepared a magnificent reward for those of you who do good.”
versets.commentaire: To the alternative posed, Qushayrī relates the exemplary reply of ʿĀʾisha: questioned by the Prophet, who asked her not to hasten and to consult her parents, she answered at once: “Is it about this that I would consult my parents? I want God, His Messenger, and the abode of the Hereafter!” — and one saw, he says, the joy upon the Prophet's face. To desire God rather than the world's splendor: such is the very condition of the iḥsān that is rewarded.

mots.racine: د و ر / أ خ ر

versets.ar: يَا نِسَاءَ النَّبِيِّ مَن يَأْتِ مِنكُنَّ بِفَاحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ يُضَاعَفْ لَهَا الْعَذَابُ ضِعْفَيْنِ ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرًا
versets.tr: yā nisāʾa n-nabiyyi man yaʾti minkunna bi-fāḥishatin mubayyinatin yuḍāʿaf lahā l-ʿadhābu ḍiʿfayni wa-kāna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīran
versets.trad: O wives of the Prophet! Whoever of you commits a manifest indecency, the punishment for her will be doubled. That is easy for God.
versets.commentaire: For Qushayrī, the increase of the penalty is itself a sign of nobility: the increase of punishment in proportion to the offense is a mark of elevation — which is why the penalty of free persons exceeds that of slaves — while its diminution is a mark of inferiority. Because the rank of the Prophet's wives surpasses that of all women, their fault weighs double, as their reward, in the next verse, will be double.

versets.ar: وَمَن يَقْنُتْ مِنكُنَّ لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتَعْمَلْ صَالِحًا نُّؤْتِهَا أَجْرَهَا مَرَّتَيْنِ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لَهَا رِزْقًا كَرِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-man yaqnut minkunna li-llāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-taʿmal ṣāliḥan nuʾtihā ajrahā marratayni wa-aʿtadnā lahā rizqan karīman
versets.trad: And whoever of you is devoted to God and His Messenger and acts righteously, We shall grant her her reward twice over, and We have prepared for her a generous provision.
versets.commentaire: As with the fault, so the merit is doubled: because their rank in nobility surpasses that of all women, He multiplied their punishment for their faults, and multiplied their reward for their obediences. Qushayrī makes the balance a justice of love: for whomever God raises in rank, He measures all double, penalty and grace, for heightened demand is the price of heightened nearness.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ك ر م

versets.ar: يَا نِسَاءَ النَّبِيِّ لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِّنَ النِّسَاءِ ۚ إِنِ اتَّقَيْتُنَّ فَلَا تَخْضَعْنَ بِالْقَوْلِ فَيَطْمَعَ الَّذِي فِي قَلْبِهِ مَرَضٌ وَقُلْنَ قَوْلًا مَّعْرُوفًا
versets.tr: yā nisāʾa n-nabiyyi lastunna ka-aḥadin mina n-nisāʾi ini ttaqaytunna fa-lā takhḍaʿna bi-l-qawli fa-yaṭmaʿa lladhī fī qalbihi maraḍun wa-qulna qawlan maʿrūfan
versets.trad: O wives of the Prophet! You are not like any other women. If you fear God, do not be too soft in speech, lest he in whose heart is a disease should covet; and speak in a fitting manner.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī summarizes briefly: He forbade them all unseemly familiarity and commanded them to guard the dignity of the Messenger, to ward off the covetousness of the hypocrites through every ambiguous softness of speech. The “fitting manner” is not coldness but right measure: a speech that honors the rank without opening a door to the sick heart.

versets.ar: وَقَرْنَ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ وَلَا تَبَرَّجْنَ تَبَرُّجَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ الْأُولَىٰ ۖ وَأَقِمْنَ الصَّلَاةَ وَآتِينَ الزَّكَاةَ وَأَطِعْنَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-qarna fī buyūtikunna wa-lā tabarrajna tabarruja l-jāhiliyyati l-ūlā wa-aqimna ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātīna z-zakāta wa-aṭiʿna llāha wa-rasūlahu innamā yurīdu llāhu li-yudhhiba ʿankumu r-rijsa ahla l-bayti wa-yuṭahhirakum taṭhīran
versets.trad: Remain in your houses, and do not flaunt yourselves as the women of the former ignorance did. Perform the prayer, give alms, and obey God and His Messenger. O People of the House, God wishes only to remove defilement from you and to purify you wholly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interprets the “defilement” (rijs) inwardly: these are the foul deeds and the base traits — the indecencies, manifest or hidden, and the vices such as avarice, greed, the severing of bonds. And the purification God wills is their reverse: He wills for them the noble traits — generosity, the preferring of others, giving, the keeping of bonds — maintains for them success, preservation, and rectitude, and purifies them of sins and faults.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرْنَ مَا يُتْلَىٰ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ مِنْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَالْحِكْمَةِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ لَطِيفًا خَبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-dhkurna mā yutlā fī buyūtikunna min āyāti llāhi wa-l-ḥikmati inna llāha kāna laṭīfan khabīran
versets.trad: O women, remember the verses of God and the wisdom recited in your houses. God is, in truth, subtle, all-aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the remembrance a recognition of the unheard-of grace that dwells in these homes: remember the immense blessing and the high condition unfolding in your houses — the descent of revelation, the coming of the angels, the sanctity of the Messenger, the light drawn even to the horizons, like the light of the sun spread over the world; acknowledge this blessing and guard this sacredness.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْلِمَاتِ وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَالْقَانِتِينَ وَالْقَانِتَاتِ وَالصَّادِقِينَ وَالصَّادِقَاتِ وَالصَّابِرِينَ وَالصَّابِرَاتِ وَالْخَاشِعِينَ وَالْخَاشِعَاتِ وَالْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ وَالْمُتَصَدِّقَاتِ وَالصَّائِمِينَ وَالصَّائِمَاتِ وَالْحَافِظِينَ فُرُوجَهُمْ وَالْحَافِظَاتِ وَالذَّاكِرِينَ اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَالذَّاكِرَاتِ أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةً وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: inna l-muslimīna wa-l-muslimāti wa-l-muʾminīna wa-l-muʾmināti wa-l-qānitīna wa-l-qānitāti wa-ṣ-ṣādiqīna wa-ṣ-ṣādiqāti wa-ṣ-ṣābirīna wa-ṣ-ṣābirāti wa-l-khāshiʿīna wa-l-khāshiʿāti wa-l-mutaṣaddiqīna wa-l-mutaṣaddiqāti wa-ṣ-ṣāʾimīna wa-ṣ-ṣāʾimāti wa-l-ḥāfiẓīna furūjahum wa-l-ḥāfiẓāti wa-dh-dhākirīna llāha kathīran wa-dh-dhākirāti aʿadda llāhu lahum maghfiratan wa-ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Indeed, the men who submit to God and the women who submit, the believing men and the believing women, the devout men and the devout women, the truthful men and the truthful women, the patient men and the patient women, the men who fear God and the women who fear, the men who give alms and the women who give, the men who fast and the women who fast, the chaste men and the chaste women, the men who remember God much and the women who remember — for them God has prepared forgiveness and a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī climbs the degrees one by one: islām is surrender, sincerity of intention, and intense striving; īmān is assent and realization, and for some the very life of the heart by God Himself; qunūt is the length of worship; ṣidq is fidelity to pacts and the guarding of limits; khushūʿ is the lowering of the inmost self at the coming of the Reality. And he crowns it with dhikr: those who invoke God with their tongues and their hearts, in all their states, without slackening and without forgetfulness penetrating them — to such belong the most beautiful wage and the most ample sequel.

mots.def: muslim: who hands himself to God.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلَا مُؤْمِنَةٍ إِذَا قَضَى اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَمْرًا أَن يَكُونَ لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِمْ ۗ وَمَن يَعْصِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلَالًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna li-muʾminin wa-lā muʾminatin idhā qaḍā llāhu wa-rasūluhu amran an yakūna lahumu l-khiyaratu min amrihim wa-man yaʿṣi llāha wa-rasūlahu fa-qad ḍalla ḍalālan mubīnan
versets.trad: It is not for a believing man or woman, when God and His Messenger have decided a matter, to exercise a choice in their affair. Whoever disobeys God and His Messenger has strayed into a manifest straying.
themes: the abandonment of one's own choice
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī warns gravely: to put oneself forward against His command, to object to His verdict, to refuse to yield to His indication — that is to knock at the door of associationism; whoever does not quickly restrain himself from it falls into its abyss. Before the decree of God and His Messenger, the believer has no “choice of his own”: the effacement of the will is the very mark of faith, and its retention, the threshold of shirk.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِي أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَاتَّقِ اللَّهَ وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللَّهُ مُبْدِيهِ وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَن تَخْشَاهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ زَيْدٌ مِّنْهَا وَطَرًا زَوَّجْنَاكَهَا لِكَيْ لَا يَكُونَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌ فِي أَزْوَاجِ أَدْعِيَائِهِمْ إِذَا قَضَوْا مِنْهُنَّ وَطَرًا ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ مَفْعُولًا
versets.tr: wa-idh taqūlu li-lladhī anʿama llāhu ʿalayhi wa-anʿamta ʿalayhi amsik ʿalayka zawjaka wa-ttaqi llāha wa-tukhfī fī nafsika mā llāhu mubdīhi wa-takhshā n-nāsa wa-llāhu aḥaqqu an takhshāhu fa-lammā qaḍā zaydun minhā waṭaran zawwajnākahā li-kay lā yakūna ʿalā l-muʾminīna ḥarajun fī azwāji adʿiyāʾihim idhā qaḍaw minhunna waṭaran wa-kāna amru llāhi mafʿūlan
versets.trad: Recall when you said to him whom God had favored and whom you had favored: “Keep your wife and fear God.” You concealed within yourself what God was to make public; you feared men, but God is worthier that you should fear Him. Then, when Zayd had ended his union with her, We gave her to you in marriage, so that there be no blame upon the believers concerning the wives of their adopted sons once they have ended their union with them. And God's command must be carried out.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains the twofold favor: God favored him by naming him and distinguishing him among the Companions by his own name; you favored him by freeing him and adopting him. And on “you feared men”: you feared for them lest they fall into trial because of Zayd's affair — this fear was compassion and mercy toward them. The verse teaches that the fear of God must take precedence over the fear of men, and that His command, once decreed, is fulfilled.

versets.ar: مَّا كَانَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ مِنْ حَرَجٍ فِيمَا فَرَضَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ۖ سُنَّةَ اللَّهِ فِي الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِن قَبْلُ ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ قَدَرًا مَّقْدُورًا
versets.tr: mā kāna ʿalā n-nabiyyi min ḥarajin fīmā faraḍa llāhu lahu sunnata llāhi fī lladhīna khalaw min qablu wa-kāna amru llāhi qadaran maqdūran
versets.trad: There is no blame upon the Prophet concerning what God has decreed for him — the way of God among those who passed before — and the command of God is a destiny decreed,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī cuts to the point: God's decree does not contradict itself nor oppose itself, is not repelled nor denied; no constraint weighs upon the Prophet, for he is preserved (maʿṣūm). What God prescribes for His Prophet belongs to His immutable way among the prophets of the past: a “measured destiny” that nothing in creation can deflect.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُبَلِّغُونَ رِسَالَاتِ اللَّهِ وَيَخْشَوْنَهُ وَلَا يَخْشَوْنَ أَحَدًا إِلَّا اللَّهَ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ حَسِيبًا
versets.tr: alladhīna yuballighūna risālāti llāhi wa-yakhshawnahu wa-lā yakhshawna aḥadan illā llāha wa-kafā bi-llāhi ḥasīban
versets.trad: those who conveyed the messages of God and feared Him, fearing none but God. And God suffices as a Reckoner.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains why the prophets fear God alone: they know that no harm, no prohibition, no adversity reaches anyone save by His decree; so they reserve fear for Him, having known that none has anything apart from Him. The exclusive fear of God is born of the knowledge that He alone acts: whoever sees the one Cause no longer trembles before the secondary causes.

versets.ar: مَّا كَانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبَا أَحَدٍ مِّن رِّجَالِكُمْ وَلَٰكِن رَّسُولَ اللَّهِ وَخَاتَمَ النَّبِيِّينَ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمًا
versets.tr: mā kāna muḥammadun abā aḥadin min rijālikum wa-lākin rasūla llāhi wa-khātama n-nabiyyīna wa-kāna llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīman
versets.trad: Muhammad is not the father of any man among you, but he is the Messenger of God and the Seal of the Prophets. And God is the perfect knower of all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates on the name: he was attached to no son, that he might have over you the tenderness of fathers; but here is what abides — he is known by me, not by his genealogy. Rarely is it said “Muhammad son of ʿAbd Allāh”; but until the end of time it will be said “Muhammad, Messenger of God.” The title of Seal effaces fleshly lineage in favor of prophetic lineage: the emblem of faith and the word of oneness, after “no god but God,” is “Muhammad is the Messenger of God.”

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ ذِكْرًا كَثِيرًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū dhkurū llāha dhikran kathīran
versets.trad: O you who believe! Remember God with much remembrance,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links invocation to love: the indication of this verse is: love God! For the Prophet said, “whoever loves a thing mentions it often.” You must then say: Allāh, then forget God no more after invoking Him. And he favors the remembrance of the heart: invoke God with your hearts, for the remembrance one can sustain unceasingly is that of the heart; as for the remembrance of the tongue, to perpetuate it without pause is well-nigh impossible.

versets.ar: وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-sabbiḥūhu bukratan wa-aṣīlan
versets.trad: and glorify Him morning and evening!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes a subtlety of language: glorification belongs to the genus of invocation, but He named it with two distinct terms — dhikr and tasbīḥ — so that weariness not overtake you. To vary the names of the same act of love is a mercy for the soul: morning and evening, the heart finds in the alternation of formulas a renewed freshness.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلَائِكَتُهُ لِيُخْرِجَكُم مِّنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ۚ وَكَانَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيمًا
versets.tr: huwa lladhī yuṣallī ʿalaykum wa-malāʾikatuhu li-yukhrijakum mina ẓ-ẓulumāti ilā n-nūri wa-kāna bi-l-muʾminīna raḥīman
versets.trad: It is He who blesses you, and His angels, to bring you out of the darknesses into the light. He is compassionate toward the believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the senses of the “bringing out”: prayer (ṣalāt) is at root invocation: God's is His call to draw us near, the angels' is their intercession for us. And “from the darknesses into the light” unfolds in degrees: from the darknesses of disbelief into the light of faith; from the darknesses of anxious self-management into the open space of contemplating the decree; from the darknesses of your souls into the lights of the inner sights in your hearts; from the causes of dispersion into the contemplation of union.

versets.ar: تَحِيَّتُهُمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُ سَلَامٌ ۚ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا كَرِيمًا
versets.tr: taḥiyyatuhum yawma yalqawnahu salāmun wa-aʿadda lahum ajran karīman
versets.trad: Their greeting, on the Day they meet Him, will be: “Peace (salām)!” He has prepared for them a generous reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in the “meeting” the direct vision: greeting, when joined to vision, and meeting, when joined to greeting, can only mean the seeing of the eyes. Salām is the word with which kings are addressed, in token of the elevation of their rank. And of the “generous reward”: their wage is filled to overflowing for a slight work, for the generous one does not reckon to the exact measure; it is the disclosure of a goodness that preceded them at the very time they were not yet.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ شَاهِدًا وَمُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu innā arsalnāka shāhidan wa-mubashshiran wa-nadhīran
versets.trad: O Prophet! We have sent you as a witness, a bearer of good tidings, and a warner,
themes: the bearer of tidings and the warner
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī resumes his titles of honor: O you ennobled by Us, We have sent you as a witness of Our oneness, announcing felicity to whoever follows Us, warning whoever transgresses Our command, teaching mankind the places of fear of Us. The Prophet is first a witness (shāhid) of tawḥīd: his mission radiates from the knowledge he has of God before instructing others.

versets.ar: وَدَاعِيًا إِلَى اللَّهِ بِإِذْنِهِ وَسِرَاجًا مُّنِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-dāʿiyan ilā llāhi bi-idhnihi wa-sirājan munīran
versets.trad: as one who calls to God by His leave, and as a luminous lamp.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī develops the image of the lamp: one who calls to Us by Us, a lamp from which clarity is drawn, a sun whose ray spreads over all who believe you and believe in you. And he draws from it an exclusivity: none reaches Us save the one who follows you and serves you, who believes you and sets you before him. The Prophet is the lamp by which the light of God reaches hearts.

mots.racine: س ر ج / ن و ر

versets.ar: وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ فَضْلًا كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-bashshiri l-muʾminīna bi-anna lahum mina llāhi faḍlan kabīran
versets.trad: Announce to the believers that a great Favor awaits them from God.
versets.commentaire: Announce to the believers Our favor upon them, the outpouring of Our largesse and Our beneficence toward them. And Qushayrī adds a tender warning: the one in whom the blessing of his faith in you has borne no fruit has no worth with Us. The great favor is not only a future promise: it is already the grace of believing in the Messenger.

versets.ar: وَلَا تُطِعِ الْكَافِرِينَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ وَدَعْ أَذَاهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-lā tuṭiʿi l-kāfirīna wa-l-munāfiqīna wa-daʿ adhāhum wa-tawakkal ʿalā llāhi wa-kafā bi-llāhi wakīlan
versets.trad: Obey neither the disbelievers nor the hypocrites, pay no heed to their hostility, and put your trust in God, for God suffices as a Trustee.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses: do not accord with those from whom We have turned away and whom We have led astray — people of disbelief and hypocrisy, people of innovation and discord — and rely on God by a continuous severance toward Him; God suffices as a guardian. Tawakkul is here presented as un-submission to the world through exclusive attachment to God: to turn from hostile men is to turn wholly toward Him.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا نَكَحْتُمُ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ ثُمَّ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ فَمَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ عِدَّةٍ تَعْتَدُّونَهَا ۖ فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā nakaḥtumu l-muʾmināti thumma ṭallaqtumūhunna min qabli an tamassūhunna fa-mā lakum ʿalayhinna min ʿiddatin taʿtaddūnahā fa-mattiʿūhunna wa-sarriḥūhunna sarāḥan jamīlan
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you marry believing women and then divorce them before having touched them, you have no waiting period to impose upon them. Give them some provision and release them in a comely manner.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the parting-gift a sense of delicacy: if you prefer the parting, grant them some provision, so that a remembrance of you remain to them in the first days of separation, until their souls settle. And on “release them comely”: mention them after the parting only for good, take back nothing of what you left with them; do not heap upon them separation by state and harm by money.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَحْلَلْنَا لَكَ أَزْوَاجَكَ اللَّاتِي آتَيْتَ أُجُورَهُنَّ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ مِمَّا أَفَاءَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمِّكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمَّاتِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَالِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَالَاتِكَ اللَّاتِي هَاجَرْنَ مَعَكَ وَامْرَأَةً مُّؤْمِنَةً إِن وَهَبَتْ نَفْسَهَا لِلنَّبِيِّ إِنْ أَرَادَ النَّبِيُّ أَن يَسْتَنكِحَهَا خَالِصَةً لَّكَ مِن دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۗ قَدْ عَلِمْنَا مَا فَرَضْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ فِي أَزْوَاجِهِمْ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُمْ لِكَيْلَا يَكُونَ عَلَيْكَ حَرَجٌ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu innā aḥlalnā laka azwājaka l-lātī ātayta ujūrahunna wa-mā malakat yamīnuka mimmā afāʾa llāhu ʿalayka wa-banāti ʿammika wa-banāti ʿammātika wa-banāti khālika wa-banāti khālātika l-lātī hājarna maʿaka wa-mraʾatan muʾminatan in wahabat nafsahā li-n-nabiyyi in arāda n-nabiyyu an yastankiḥahā khāliṣatan laka min dūni l-muʾminīna qad ʿalimnā mā faraḍnā ʿalayhim fī azwājihim wa-mā malakat aymānuhum li-kaylā yakūna ʿalayka ḥarajun wa-kāna llāhu ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: O Prophet! We have made lawful for you the wives to whom you have given their dowry, the captives God has assigned to you, the daughters of your paternal uncle, the daughters of your paternal aunts, the daughters of your maternal uncle, the daughters of your maternal aunts who emigrated with you, and any believing woman who would devote herself to the Prophet, provided the Prophet wished to marry her — a privilege granted to you, to the exclusion of the other believers. We know what We have imposed upon them concerning their wives and their slaves, so that there be no grievance against you. God is forgiving, compassionate!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains the latitude granted: We widened for you the matter of marriage as much as you will, for you are safe from the flaw of failing in equity between them, of failing to observe their rights, or of wronging them. And he sees in it a sign of nobility: the widening in matters of marriage bears witness to virtue, as the distinction between the free man and the slave. The privilege is not license but the mark of the Prophet's singular rank, preserved from all injustice.

mots.def: khāliṣa: what is reserved as one's own.

versets.ar: تُرْجِي مَن تَشَاءُ مِنْهُنَّ وَتُؤْوِي إِلَيْكَ مَن تَشَاءُ ۖ وَمَنِ ابْتَغَيْتَ مِمَّنْ عَزَلْتَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰ أَن تَقَرَّ أَعْيُنُهُنَّ وَلَا يَحْزَنَّ وَيَرْضَيْنَ بِمَا آتَيْتَهُنَّ كُلُّهُنَّ ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَلِيمًا
versets.tr: turjī man tashāʾu minhunna wa-tuʾwī ilayka man tashāʾu wa-mani btaghayta mimman ʿazalta fa-lā junāḥa ʿalayka dhālika adnā an taqarra aʿyunuhunna wa-lā yaḥzanna wa-yarḍayna bimā ātaytahunna kulluhunna wa-llāhu yaʿlamu mā fī qulūbikum wa-kāna llāhu ʿalīman ḥalīman
versets.trad: You may defer whom you will of them, and keep with you whom you will; and if you seek again one of those you had set aside, there is no blame upon you. That is most apt to gladden them, to spare them sorrow, and to content them all with what you grant. God knows what is in your hearts. God is all-knowing, forbearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī summarizes the freedom left to the Prophet: whom you will — according to what your will attaches to and where your choice falls — no blame nor fault upon you. He notes that this latitude aims at the soothing of hearts: that their eyes be refreshed, that they not grieve, and that they all be content. And the verse is sealed on the divine knowledge of hearts: God knows what hides therein, and remains full of forbearance (ḥalīm).

mots.racine: ق ر ر / ع ي ن

versets.ar: لَّا يَحِلُّ لَكَ النِّسَاءُ مِن بَعْدُ وَلَا أَن تَبَدَّلَ بِهِنَّ مِنْ أَزْوَاجٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ حُسْنُهُنَّ إِلَّا مَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ رَّقِيبًا
versets.tr: lā yaḥillu laka n-nisāʾu min baʿdu wa-lā an tabaddala bihinna min azwājin wa-law aʿjabaka ḥusnuhunna illā mā malakat yamīnuka wa-kāna llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin raqīban
versets.trad: It is no longer lawful for you to marry other women nor to take others in exchange for yours, even if their beauty charms you, save those whom your right hand possesses. God watches perfectly over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads here a reciprocity of honor: because they chose you, God established for them a sacredness; as they chose you, do not choose another woman in their place, to soothe their hearts and establish an equity between you and them. And he concludes on a maxim: this bears witness to his nobility — for fidelity is nobility and religion.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتَ النَّبِيِّ إِلَّا أَن يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلَىٰ طَعَامٍ غَيْرَ نَاظِرِينَ إِنَاهُ وَلَٰكِنْ إِذَا دُعِيتُمْ فَادْخُلُوا فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَانتَشِرُوا وَلَا مُسْتَأْنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِي النَّبِيَّ فَيَسْتَحْيِي مِنكُمْ ۖ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَسْتَحْيِي مِنَ الْحَقِّ ۚ وَإِذَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ مَتَاعًا فَاسْأَلُوهُنَّ مِن وَرَاءِ حِجَابٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَطْهَرُ لِقُلُوبِكُمْ وَقُلُوبِهِنَّ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَن تُؤْذُوا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَلَا أَن تَنكِحُوا أَزْوَاجَهُ مِن بَعْدِهِ أَبَدًا ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ عِندَ اللَّهِ عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tadkhulū buyūta n-nabiyyi illā an yuʾdhana lakum ilā ṭaʿāmin ghayra nāẓirīna ināhu wa-lākin idhā duʿītum fa-dkhulū fa-idhā ṭaʿimtum fa-ntashirū wa-lā mustaʾnisīna li-ḥadīthin inna dhālikum kāna yuʾdhī n-nabiyya fa-yastaḥyī minkum wa-llāhu lā yastaḥyī mina l-ḥaqqi wa-idhā saʾaltumūhunna matāʿan fa-sʾalūhunna min warāʾi ḥijābin dhālikum aṭharu li-qulūbikum wa-qulūbihinna wa-mā kāna lakum an tuʾdhū rasūla llāhi wa-lā an tankiḥū azwājahu min baʿdihi abadan inna dhālikum kāna ʿinda llāhi ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not enter the houses of the Prophet for a meal until you are permitted, and do not linger waiting for the meal to be ready. But when you are invited, enter, and when you have eaten, disperse, without entering into familiar conversation. This incommodes the Prophet, but he is ashamed to tell you so. God, however, is not ashamed of the truth. When you ask the Prophet's women for some favor, do so from behind a veil. That is purer for your hearts and for their hearts. You must not offend the Messenger of God, nor ever marry his former wives: that would be, on your part, a most grievous fault before God.
themes: the veil and the purity of hearts
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the protocol a school of adab: He commanded them to keep good manners in seeking permission, to respect the time and the duty of regard; once entered, to bear themselves with courtesy; their needs met, to withdraw; let the beauty of his character not make you forget the adab. And on the veil: that is purer for your hearts and theirs — He moved them from familiar habit to the known of the Law, showing that man remains man, even among the Companions: let none deem himself safe from his own soul.

versets.ar: إِن تُبْدُوا شَيْئًا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمًا
versets.tr: in tubdū shayʾan aw tukhfūhu fa-inna llāha kāna bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīman
versets.trad: Whether you reveal a thing or conceal it, God knows all things perfectly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the divine omniscience an inner exigency: to keep the heart with God, to observe the command — between oneself and God — in uprightness and the permanence of the moments: none has the strength for this save the elite of the people of presence. Since God sees the secret as the manifest, true vigilance is not to watch one's acts alone, but to keep one's inmost self pure at every hour.

versets.ar: لَّا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِي آبَائِهِنَّ وَلَا أَبْنَائِهِنَّ وَلَا إِخْوَانِهِنَّ وَلَا أَبْنَاءِ إِخْوَانِهِنَّ وَلَا أَبْنَاءِ أَخَوَاتِهِنَّ وَلَا نِسَائِهِنَّ وَلَا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُنَّ ۗ وَاتَّقِينَ اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدًا
versets.tr: lā junāḥa ʿalayhinna fī ābāʾihinna wa-lā abnāʾihinna wa-lā ikhwānihinna wa-lā abnāʾi ikhwānihinna wa-lā abnāʾi akhawātihinna wa-lā nisāʾihinna wa-lā mā malakat aymānuhunna wa-ttaqīna llāha inna llāha kāna ʿalā kulli shayʾin shahīdan
versets.trad: There is no blame upon the women [to appear unveiled] before their fathers, their sons, their brothers, the sons of their brothers, the sons of their sisters, their womenfolk, and those their right hands possess. Let them fear God! God is, in truth, witness over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls the occasion: when the verse of the veil came down, it bore hard upon the wives, the women, and the men; God then sent down this verse as an alleviation, permitting the sight of these kin according to the detail of the Law. But the verse is sealed on “let them fear God”: the legal alleviation does not abolish inner vigilance, for God remains “witness over all things.”

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ۚ يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا
versets.tr: inna llāha wa-malāʾikatahu yuṣallūna ʿalā n-nabiyyi yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ṣallū ʿalayhi wa-sallimū taslīman
versets.trad: Indeed, God and His angels bless the Prophet. O you who believe, bless him and greet him with peace.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils the reciprocity: God willed that the community have, with Him, toward the Prophet a hand of service, as he has toward it, through intercession, a hand of bounty; so He commanded them to bless him. And he sees in it a vertiginous indication: even the highest of ranks — that of the Messenger — does not dispense with the increase come from God; he needed the increase of his community's blessings upon him, a sign that none, at any instant, is self-sufficient of God.

mots.def: ṣallā ʿalā: to bless, to pray upon.
sallama: to give the salutation of peace.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ لَعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yuʾdhūna llāha wa-rasūlahu laʿanahumu llāhu fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-aʿadda lahum ʿadhāban muhīnan
versets.trad: Those who offend God and His Messenger, God curses them in this world and the next, and prepares for them a humiliating punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī establishes the bond of honor between God and His Messenger: one offends God by the sins that call down punishment, and one offends His friends; and since “to obey the Messenger is to obey God,” to offend His Messenger and His prophets is to offend Him. The sense, he says, is the affirmation of the Prophet's sublime rank: to harm him is to assail God Himself.

mots.def: laʿana: to curse, to exclude from mercy.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ بِغَيْرِ مَا اكْتَسَبُوا فَقَدِ احْتَمَلُوا بُهْتَانًا وَإِثْمًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yuʾdhūna l-muʾminīna wa-l-muʾmināti bi-ghayri mā ktasabū fa-qadi ḥtamalū buhtānan wa-ithman mubīnan
versets.trad: And those who offend the believing men and women without their having deserved it bear an infamy and a manifest sin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī marks the hierarchy of sanctities: He coupled the offense against the Messenger with that against God, then mentioned the offense against the believers — which indicates that the rank of the believers is below that of the Messenger. Yet to offend the innocent believer remains “infamy and manifest sin”: the dignity of the believer, though less than the Prophet's, remains protected by God.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَاجِكَ وَبَنَاتِكَ وَنِسَاءِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ يُدْنِينَ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِن جَلَابِيبِهِنَّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰ أَن يُعْرَفْنَ فَلَا يُؤْذَيْنَ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu qul li-azwājika wa-banātika wa-nisāʾi l-muʾminīna yudnīna ʿalayhinna min jalābībihinna dhālika adnā an yuʿrafna fa-lā yuʾdhayna wa-kāna llāhu ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: O Prophet! Tell your wives, your daughters, and the women of the believers to draw over themselves a part of their cloaks. Thus they will more readily be recognized and will not be offended. God is forgiving, compassionate!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads this verse as a protective admonition: it is a reminder for them to keep their sacredness and affirm their rank, a safeguard for them, and a command to preserve themselves and remain chaste. The garment is not constraint but a sign of honor: the cloak distinguishes and protects, warding off offense from those whose rank is high.

mots.def: adnā: to draw near, to let down.

versets.ar: لَّئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ الْمُنَافِقُونَ وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ وَالْمُرْجِفُونَ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ بِهِمْ ثُمَّ لَا يُجَاوِرُونَكَ فِيهَا إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: la-in lam yantahi l-munāfiqūna wa-lladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun wa-l-murjifūna fī l-madīnati la-nughriyannaka bihim thumma lā yujāwirūnaka fīhā illā qalīlan
versets.trad: If the hypocrites, those in whose hearts is a disease, and those who spread sedition in Medina do not cease their doings, We shall set you against them, and they will no longer be your neighbors in this city for long.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī joins this warning to the protection of the believing women: He coupled with the reminder of their chastity the threat against the hypocrites, in their undertaking of all that troubled the heart of the Messenger — their alarming rumors in Medina. If they do not cease, he says, We would apply against them Our way of destruction upon the disbelievers of old. The malicious rumor is no trifle: it draws down the sanction of God.

mots.sens: We shall set you against them

versets.ar: مَّلْعُونِينَ ۖ أَيْنَمَا ثُقِفُوا أُخِذُوا وَقُتِّلُوا تَقْتِيلًا
versets.tr: malʿūnīna aynamā thuqifū ukhidhū wa-quttilū taqtīlan
versets.trad: They shall be cursed. Wherever they are found, they will be seized and put to death.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ranges this verse within the immutable way of God toward the corrupters: the curse is the distancing from mercy, the consequence of obstinate corruption. Whoever sows discord and does not repent of it excludes himself from divine protection, and the sunna of God, which struck the destroyers of old, knows no change.

mots.def: thaqifa: to catch up, to seize.

versets.ar: سُنَّةَ اللَّهِ فِي الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ اللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا
versets.tr: sunnata llāhi fī lladhīna khalaw min qablu wa-lan tajida li-sunnati llāhi tabdīlan
versets.trad: Such is the way that God established for those who lived before, and you will find no change in the way of God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī confirms the immutability of the decree: had they not abstained from the rumor and the like, We would apply against them Our way in the annihilation of those who preceded. The sunnat Allāh is the constant law of His justice: it neither alters nor is substituted, striking yesterday's corrupter as today's.

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُكَ النَّاسُ عَنِ السَّاعَةِ ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ اللَّهِ ۚ وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ السَّاعَةَ تَكُونُ قَرِيبًا
versets.tr: yasʾaluka n-nāsu ʿani s-sāʿati qul innamā ʿilmuhā ʿinda llāhi wa-mā yudrīka laʿalla s-sāʿata takūnu qarīban
versets.trad: People question you about the Hour: say, “God alone has knowledge of it.” And what would make you know? Perhaps the Hour is near.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports that people would question about the Hour while denying it, then press for its coming without preparing for it. The knowledge of the Hour, sealed in God alone, is a mercy: its veil invites not to curiosity but to vigilance. That the Hour “be near” should suffice to awaken the servant's heart, who must hold himself ready rather than probe its date.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَعَنَ الْكَافِرِينَ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ سَعِيرًا
versets.tr: inna llāha laʿana l-kāfirīna wa-aʿadda lahum saʿīran
versets.trad: Indeed, God has cursed the disbelievers and prepared for them a blaze,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, in continuation of the mention of the Hour, recalls the severity of the punishment God knows He has in store for them, and the regret that will seize them for what they neglected. The curse here is the definitive distancing from mercy: those who have veiled the truth (kafara, “to cover”) find themselves in turn enveloped by the fire they had prepared for themselves.

mots.def: kāfir: who denies, veils the truth.

versets.ar: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۖ لَّا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhā abadan lā yajidūna waliyyan wa-lā naṣīran
versets.trad: in which they will abide forever, finding neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: The fate of those who refused the sole Protector is to find themselves, in the next world, without any protector or helper. Qushayrī had already posed the question at verse 17: “who then will shield you from God?” Here the answer hardens: having sought supports apart from God, they remain forever bereft of all support, for none is walī or naṣīr save Him.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تُقَلَّبُ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِي النَّارِ يَقُولُونَ يَا لَيْتَنَا أَطَعْنَا اللَّهَ وَأَطَعْنَا الرَّسُولَا
versets.tr: yawma tuqallabu wujūhuhum fī n-nāri yaqūlūna yā laytanā aṭaʿnā llāha wa-aṭaʿnā r-rasūlā
versets.trad: On the Day their faces are turned about in the Fire, they will say: “Woe to us! Had we only obeyed God and obeyed the Messenger!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī evokes the regret that will seize them for what they neglected. The wish “had we only obeyed” comes too late: it is the cry of the soul that discovers, in the fire, the truth it had veiled. Obedience to God and the Messenger, refused in the time of trial, becomes the object of a vain longing when the time of action is past.

mots.def: qallaba: to turn over, to turn about.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا رَبَّنَا إِنَّا أَطَعْنَا سَادَتَنَا وَكُبَرَاءَنَا فَأَضَلُّونَا السَّبِيلَا
versets.tr: wa-qālū rabbanā innā aṭaʿnā sādatanā wa-kubarāʾanā fa-aḍallūnā s-sabīlā
versets.trad: And they will say: “Our Lord! We obeyed our chiefs and our great ones, and they led us astray from the path.
versets.commentaire: The regret of the damned denounces blind obedience to chiefs at the expense of obedience to God. Qushayrī sees in the confession an unveiling of the root of their loss: having preferred the following of the world's “great ones” to the divine indication. But casting the fault upon the leaders does not save: each answers for his own choice to have followed without discernment.

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا آتِهِمْ ضِعْفَيْنِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ وَالْعَنْهُمْ لَعْنًا كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: rabbanā ātihim ḍiʿfayni mina l-ʿadhābi wa-lʿanhum laʿnan kabīran
versets.trad: Our Lord! Send them a double punishment and cast upon them a great curse!”
versets.commentaire: At the height of regret, the misled turn against those who misled them and call down upon them a double punishment. Qushayrī ranges this cry among the vain lamentations of the next world: it reveals that the one who misleads and the one who lets himself be misled are bound in the same loss. The mutual hatred of the damned is the bitter fruit of an obedience that should have gone to God alone.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ آذَوْا مُوسَىٰ فَبَرَّأَهُ اللَّهُ مِمَّا قَالُوا ۚ وَكَانَ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَجِيهًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā takūnū ka-lladhīna ādhaw mūsā fa-barraʾahu llāhu mimmā qālū wa-kāna ʿinda llāhi wajīhan
versets.trad: O you who believe! Be not like those who offended Moses. God cleared him of what they said, and he held an honorable place with God.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports that the children of Israel suspected Moses of a defect, for he was modest and would not bare himself; God made the rock flee with his garments, disclosing his sound body and his innocence. And he draws a lesson on true dignity: the consideration that profits is the one held with God, not with men; the approval of people is without worth or weight — especially that of the common folk, who accept without reason and reject without reason.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-qūlū qawlan sadīdan
versets.trad: O you who believe, fear God and speak with uprightness;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interprets the “upright word” by the sincerity of testimony: the right word is the word of sincerity, the two attestations issuing from a truthful conscience. And he adds: the uprightness of your words is the uprightness of your works. The tongue is the mirror of the heart: whoever fears God speaks rightly, and the rectitude of the saying prepares the rectitude of the doing.

versets.ar: يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ ۗ وَمَن يُطِعِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَقَدْ فَازَ فَوْزًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: yuṣliḥ lakum aʿmālakum wa-yaghfir lakum dhunūbakum wa-man yuṭiʿi llāha wa-rasūlahu fa-qad fāza fawzan ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: He will make your deeds more worthy and forgive your sins. Whoever obeys God and His Messenger has attained an ineffable felicity.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shows the grace of the sincere word: whoever contents himself with the attestation — the word that abandons association — and says it with sincerity, God rectifies for him his works of this world of every flaw and forgives him in the next his faults; thus the felicity of both abodes comes to him. And he notes a significant order: He mentioned the works before the forgiveness, for so long as your works and your affairs are not set right, you are not free for the affair of your Hereafter.

versets.ar: إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا الْأَمَانَةَ عَلَى السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالْجِبَالِ فَأَبَيْنَ أَن يَحْمِلْنَهَا وَأَشْفَقْنَ مِنْهَا وَحَمَلَهَا الْإِنسَانُ ۖ إِنَّهُ كَانَ ظَلُومًا جَهُولًا
versets.tr: innā ʿaraḍnā l-amānata ʿalā s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-l-jibāli fa-abayna an yaḥmilnahā wa-ashfaqna minhā wa-ḥamalahā l-insānu innahu kāna ẓalūman jahūlan
versets.trad: We offered the Trust to the heavens, the earth, and the mountains, but they refused to bear it; they were afraid of it, and man bore it. Yet he proved unjust, ignorant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds many senses of the Trust: “they refused to bear it” means they refused to betray in it; “man bore it” means he betrayed in it. He specifies the nature of the refusal: a refusal of reverent awe, not of pride; they begged off, and He exempted them. He gives its definitions: the amāna is professed tawḥīd and the guarding of limits through striving; it is the fulfilling of duties, their roots and their branches. And this pointed remark: man bore it by God, not by himself; God disclosed Himself to the heavens and the earth by His Lordship and Majesty — they took fright; He disclosed Himself to Adam by His gentleness — he accepted and bore.

mots.def: ashfaqa: to fear out of reverence.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ج ه ل
mots.def: ẓalūm: most unjust; jahūl: most ignorant.

versets.ar: لِّيُعَذِّبَ اللَّهُ الْمُنَافِقِينَ وَالْمُنَافِقَاتِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ وَالْمُشْرِكَاتِ وَيَتُوبَ اللَّهُ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: li-yuʿadhdhiba llāhu l-munāfiqīna wa-l-munāfiqāti wa-l-mushrikīna wa-l-mushrikāti wa-yatūba llāhu ʿalā l-muʾminīna wa-l-muʾmināti wa-kāna llāhu ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: So that God may punish the hypocrites, men and women, and the associators, men and women, and that He may turn in mercy to the believers, men and women. God is forgiving, compassionate!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the sūra on a door of hope — the only time, he notes, that he adds a remark after the close of a sūra. The verse names the hypocrites and associators punished, and the believers to whom God turns. Where then are the worshippers and their mention?, he asks. And he answers: they are included among those to whom He turns, in the mass of believing sinners toward whom He turns. O you the sinner, you feared the worshippers would cast you out of their number: behold how the All-Mighty, in this discourse, has ranged you among them!

mots.def: mushrik: who associates partners with God.
tāba ʿalā: to turn with mercy.
